question: [File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: subscribing()] Can an unprivileged external user submit transactions that cause the mirror node's gRPC API to disconnect subscribers, causing the GrpcSubscription rate to drop to zero in subscribing(), and the health indicator to return DOWN/UNKNOWN even though the network is processing transactions normally?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: publishing()] Can an unprivileged external user submit transactions with future valid start times that conflict with the monitor's transaction IDs, causing TRANSACTION_EXPIRED or INVALID_TRANSACTION_START errors that reduce the publish rate to zero in publishing()?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: health()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the monitor's health endpoint (/actuator/health) to be polled at a very high rate, causing the reactive chain in health() to be subscribed to concurrently, and the static CLUSTER_UP to be written by multiple threads simultaneously, leading to inconsistent health metric values?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: restNetworkStakeHealth()] Can an unprivileged external user submit transactions that cause the mirror node's REST Java API to return HTTP 503 (Service Unavailable) for /network/stake, causing restNetworkStakeHealth() to return Status.DOWN and suppress all publish/subscribe health evaluation, creating a false cluster-down state?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: publishing()] Can an unprivileged external user submit transactions that cause the consensus node to return PLATFORM_NOT_ACTIVE errors, which are tracked as publish failures, causing the publish rate to drop to zero in publishing() and triggering a false DOWN/UNKNOWN health status?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: subscribing()] Can an unprivileged external user submit transactions that cause the REST subscriber to fail to retrieve transaction records (e.g., by causing the mirror node to return 404 for the transaction ID), driving the REST subscription rate to zero in subscribing() and causing a false DOWN/UNKNOWN health status?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: health()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that health() does not authenticate or rate-limit callers to poll the health endpoint at high frequency, causing the reactive pipeline to be subscribed thousands of times per second, exhausting thread pool resources and causing a denial of service?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: publishing()] Can an unprivileged external user submit transactions that cause the monitor's operator account to be flagged as invalid (e.g., by deleting the account if possible), causing all publish scenarios to fail with INVALID_ACCOUNT_ID errors and the publish rate to drop to zero in publishing()?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: restNetworkStakeHealth()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the error message format in restNetworkStakeHealth() — String.format('Network stake status is %s with error: %s', status, e.getMessage()) — by causing an exception with a crafted message that is logged at ERROR level, flooding logs and causing log storage exhaustion?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: subscribing()] Can an unprivileged external user submit transactions that cause the mirror node's gRPC API to return INVALID_ARGUMENT errors, which are not retried by GrpcSubscriber.shouldRetry(), causing the gRPC subscription to permanently stop and the rate in subscribing() to drop to zero?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: health()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the monitor's publish rate to drop to zero by submitting transactions that consume all available network TPS quota, causing publishing() to return a DOWN/UNKNOWN health signal that triggers automated cluster failover?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: publishing()] Can an unprivileged external user submit transactions with the same transaction ID as the monitor's operator, causing DUPLICATE_TRANSACTION rejections that reduce the publish rate to zero in publishing() and trigger a false DOWN health status?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: recordHealthMetric()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the health() reactive chain to complete without emitting any item (empty Mono), triggering the doOnNext(this::recordHealthMetric) to never fire, leaving CLUSTER_UP in a stale state that does not reflect the current cluster health?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: restNetworkStakeHealth()] Can an unprivileged external user submit transactions that cause the mirror node's database to lag, making the /network/stake endpoint return stale data with a 2xx status, causing restNetworkStakeHealth() to return UP even though the network is actually degraded?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: publishing()] Can an unprivileged external user submit transactions that cause the consensus node to return BUSY errors, which are treated as publish failures, causing the publish rate to drop to zero in publishing() and triggering a false DOWN/UNKNOWN health status?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: health()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the health() method re-evaluates restNetworkStakeHealth() on every call without caching, by causing the /network/stake endpoint to alternate between 2xx and 5xx responses, making the health status oscillate and creating alert noise?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: subscribing()] Can an unprivileged external user submit transactions that cause the mirror node's REST API to return 429 (Too Many Requests) for transaction lookups, causing the REST subscriber to exhaust its retry attempts and the subscription rate to drop to zero in subscribing()?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: publishing()] Can an unprivileged external user submit transactions that cause the monitor's publish scenario to reach its configured limit (PublishScenarioProperties.limit), causing a ScenarioException that disables the scenario and drives the rate to zero in publishing(), resulting in a false DOWN/UNKNOWN health status?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: health()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the monitor's health endpoint to return Status.DOWN by submitting transactions that cause the network stake endpoint to return 5xx, without needing to affect any actual transaction publishing or subscribing, since restNetworkStakeHealth() short-circuits the entire health() evaluation?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: restNetworkStakeHealth()] Can an unprivileged external user submit transactions that cause the mirror node's REST Java API to be restarted, causing a ConnectException in restNetworkStakeHealth() that is classified as Status.DOWN, triggering a false cluster-down alert?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: subscribing()] Can an unprivileged external user submit transactions that cause the mirror node's gRPC API to return a stream of errors, causing the GrpcSubscriber to enter a retry loop that never successfully receives messages, keeping the subscription rate at zero in subscribing()?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: health()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that health() uses a static Mono<Health> UP/DOWN/UNKNOWN (pre-built at class load time) to cause a scenario where the health detail message is always empty for UP status, hiding diagnostic information that would reveal a tampered transaction state?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: publishing()] Can an unprivileged external user submit transactions that cause the consensus node to return INVALID_SIGNATURE errors for the monitor's transactions, causing the publish rate to drop to zero in publishing() and triggering a false DOWN/UNKNOWN health status?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: restNetworkStakeHealth()] Can an unprivileged external user submit transactions that cause the mirror node's REST Java API to return HTTP 404 for /network/stake (not a 2xx or 5xx), causing restNetworkStakeHealth() to return Status.UNKNOWN and bypass the publish/subscribe health checks, masking real transaction failures?
